commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR passes a remaining startup budget into Codex app-server client initialization, adds an initialize-specific startup wrapper and retry guard, and expands Codex startup timeout regression tests.

PR surface: Source +40, Tests +52. Total +92 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect by source inspection: the existing never-resolving factory regression expects `codex app-server startup timed out`, while the PR wraps that factory promise with an initialize-timeout message. I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair can keep the remaining-budget initialize fix while restoring the generic startup-timeout behavior for pre-initialize factory hangs.

target.title: fix #84567: [Bug]: Codex bundled harness initialize still hangs in 2026.5.18 isolated cron — surfaces via #64744 timeout-wrapping as 'isolated agent setup timed out before runner start'
target.body:
## Summary

- Bound Codex app-server `initialize` with the remaining startup budget instead of giving it the full outer timeout after earlier startup work has already elapsed.
- Make the initialize phase timeout fire before the outer startup watchdog, so stalled initialize reports `codex app-server initialize timed out` instead of the generic startup timeout.
- Stop connection-closed retry attempts after the outer startup timeout has already abandoned the startup operation.
- Add regression coverage for the stalled-initialize stdio path, remaining-budget calculation, startup-timeout cleanup, cancellation cleanup, and startup RPC timeout behavior.

Fixes #84567.

## Root Cause

The Codex startup operation had two timeout layers: the outer app-server startup watchdog and the inner shared-client initialize timeout. The inner initialize timeout was created later but still received the full startup timeout duration, so both timers could race at the same effective boundary and the outer watchdog could surface the less-specific `codex app-server startup timed out` / cron setup timeout. When the outer timeout closed the client, the background startup attempt could also still treat that connection close as retryable.

## Why this is root cause fix

`initialize` is the first required Codex app-server protocol step before `thread/start`, so the startup invariant is that this phase must either complete or fail inside the same startup deadline. This patch enforces that invariant at the source boundary: the initialize phase now receives only the remaining startup budget, its phase timer is scheduled to win before the outer watchdog, and the retry loop no longer restarts after the outer startup timeout has already abandoned the attempt. That changes the failing boundary itself rather than masking the later cron diagnostic.

## Why it matters / User impact

Isolated cron users could see `isolated agent setup timed out before runner start` even though the real stall was the Codex app-server `initialize` handshake. That made the failure hard to diagnose and could leave an abandoned startup path racing in the background. With this patch, the user-visible failure points at the stalled Codex initialize phase and cleanup starts before a thread runner is created.

## Fix classification

Root cause fix.

## Maintainer-ready confidence

High for the pre-`thread/start` initialize stall boundary: the code now enforces the Codex lifecycle deadline where the protocol can stall, the regression test covers the timer ordering case, and the real-stdio proof shows the initialize error wins over the generic startup watchdog. The only remaining proof gap is a live OpenAI OAuth isolated cron on the reporter's account, which is unavailable in this environment and is disclosed below.

## Maintainer-ready checklist

- **Root cause:** The bug was a timeout-budget invariant mismatch in Codex startup. OpenClaw started the outer app-server startup watchdog before the initialize phase, but the later initialize timeout still received the full startup timeout duration. In the stalled-stdio case, that allowed the generic outer watchdog to win the race even though the real blocked protocol step was `initialize`, and the background startup attempt could still treat the watchdog-induced connection close as retryable.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** Codex requires `initialize` before `thread/start`, so the first protocol boundary must complete or fail inside the same startup deadline. This patch derives the initialize timeout from the remaining startup deadline, makes that phase timeout fire before the outer watchdog, closes late factory results without swallowing the original error, and prevents connection-closed retries once the startup watchdog has already abandoned the attempt. Successful initialize responses still follow the same path; only the stalled boundary now reports the correct phase.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Isolated cron users get a precise Codex initialize timeout instead of a generic pre-run setup timeout, making the stalled provider setup diagnosable before any runner starts.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the pre-`thread/start` initialize stall boundary; the live OAuth account path is not available here and is disclosed in the proof section.
- **Patch quality notes:** Timeout/retry and broad-catch warnings are intentional and bounded: the catch only closes late factory results and rethrows, while the retry guard only applies after the startup watchdog has already fired.
- **Target test file:** `extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 / RF-002 / RF-004 / RF-005:** Added copied real-stdio proof below. The proof starts a real child process over stdio through `CodexAppServerClient.start()`, lets it receive `initialize`, intentionally never replies, and shows OpenClaw now surfaces `codex app-server initialize timed out` before the generic startup watchdog.
- **RF-003 / RF-006:** `extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.ts` now passes a remaining startup budget to initialize, wraps the factory promise with the same phase timeout, and blocks post-timeout connection-close retries.

## Codex contract checked

Checked sibling `../codex` source before changing the Codex boundary:

- `codex-rs/app-server/README.md:76` and `codex-rs/app-server/README.md:85` document that clients must send `initialize` before any other method and then send `initialized`.
- `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/common.rs:435` through `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/common.rs:449` define the `initialize` and `thread/start` client requests as distinct protocol steps.
- `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/initialize_processor.rs:59` through `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/initialize_processor.rs:61` reject repeated initialize calls, and `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/initialize_processor.rs:148` through `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/initialize_processor.rs:153` send the initialize response before marking outbound initialized.
- `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/thread_processor.rs:377` through `codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/thread_processor.rs:394` handle `thread/start` after initialization as the next lifecycle phase.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** stalled real stdio Codex initialize is bounded before `thread/start` and reports the initialize phase error.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux, Node `v22.22.0`, OpenClaw worktree after this patch, with a real stdio child process spawned through the Codex app-server client startup path.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
pnpm exec tsx <evidence>/real-stdio-stalled-initialize-proof.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:** copied output from the real-stdio proof:

```text
Behavior addressed: stalled real stdio Codex initialize is bounded before thread/start.
Real environment tested: Linux Node v22.22.0; spawned /usr/local/node/bin/node <evidence>/stalled-codex-app-server.mjs
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm exec tsx evidence/real-stdio-stalled-initialize-proof.ts
Evidence after fix: observed error "codex app-server initialize timed out" after 300 ms; onStartupTimeoutCalls=0; stubLog=<evidence>/stalled-codex-stub.log
Observed result after fix: initialize phase error won; generic codex app-server startup timed out was not surfaced.
What was not tested: a live OpenAI OAuth account or real Codex binary responding successfully after initialize.
```

Copied stub log from the real stdio child:

```text
stub-start pid=<redacted>
received method=initialize id=1
stub-exit signal=stdin-end
```

- **Observed result after fix:** the stdio child received the real `initialize` request, did not answer, OpenClaw raised `codex app-server initialize timed out`, and the generic `codex app-server startup timed out` message did not win.
- **What was not tested:** a live OpenAI OAuth isolated cron on the reporter's account. This environment does not have that OAuth profile, so the live proof uses a deterministic real-stdio stalled app-server child to exercise the same pre-`thread/start` initialize boundary without external credentials.

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx <evidence>/real-stdio-stalled-initialize-proof.ts`

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 6 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-startup.test.ts`
  - Result: no lint findings.
- `pnpm exec tsx <evidence>/real-stdio-stalled-initialize-proof.ts`
  - Result: copied in **Real behavior proof** above.

## Patch quality notes

- **Timeout/retry change:** This intentionally changes timeout ordering, not the configured timeout amount. The initialize phase receives the remaining startup budget and a 50ms head start so its specific error wins before the outer watchdog. The `2_000ms` value in `attempt-startup.test.ts` is only a test cleanup wait, not a production timeout.
- **Broad catch warning:** The new `.catch()` only attaches cleanup to a late-resolving factory promise and immediately rethrows the original error. It does not swallow, translate, retry, or fallback from failures.
- **Compatibility / public contract:** No user config, schema, protocol payload, or public app-server API changes. The existing PR already added an optional factory timeout argument; callers that omit it keep the previous default behavior. This patch only changes the Codex startup caller to pass the remaining budget.
- **Related open PR scan:** `init-pr` and `check-upstream-fixed` checked the existing PR against current `origin/main`; main does not already cover this repair, and there is no overlap between current main changes and this PR's changed files. No broader canonical open PR was identified for this private Codex startup timeout boundary.
- **Out of scope:** This does not change live OAuth auth selection, model routing, successful Codex app-server startup behavior, or post-`thread/start` runtime behavior.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `availability`, `compatibility`.
- **Risk explanation:** Availability risk is limited to Codex app-server startup: a stalled initialize now fails earlier with `codex app-server initialize timed out` inside the already configured startup budget. Compatibility risk is limited to the optional factory timeout path already present on this PR; there is no new config/schema/protocol surface.
- **Why acceptable:** The change narrows an ambiguous startup timeout to the required first protocol phase and prevents retries only after the outer startup watchdog has already abandoned the attempt. It does not introduce a fallback, broaden retries, or change successful startup behavior.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
